title: Nothocalais troximoides
intro: Nothocalais troximoides, also known as Western yarrow or Western false yarrow, is a perennial herb native to western North America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as prevent the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The bioactive constituents of Nothocalais troximoides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Nothocalais troximoides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues.
taxonomy: Nothocalais troximoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It is native to the mountains of the western United States, specifically in the states of Arizona, California, Colorado, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, New Mexico, Oregon, Utah, Washington, and Wyoming. Nothocalais troximoides is closely related to other species within the genus Nothocalais. The plant is characterized by its yellow, four-petaled flowers and its preference for well-drained, rocky soils. As part of the Ranunculaceae family, Nothocalais troximoides is classified as a type of buttercup.
